reasoning: FALSE POSITIVE. The finding claims $settings['before_language_code'] and $settings['after_language_code'] are echoed without escaping, but this is incorrect. The two settings are concatenated into $language_code via sprintf() at lines 753-758 (no escaping at construction, correct), but the ONLY output point for $language_code is line 798: esc_html( $language_code ). The esc_html() call wraps the entire composed string — including the attacker-controlled before/after fragments — and converts HTML special characters to entities before output. A payload like <script>alert(1)</script> stored in either setting would render as <script>alert(1)</script> in the browser, which is inert. grep confirms there are exactly four references to these setting names in the entire plugin: two at control registration (lines 673, 681) and two at value interpolation (lines 755, 757); neither of the latter is output directly — both feed $language_code which is consumed only inside esc_html() on line 798. No alternative unescaped output path exists.
